entries.title: Commentary on the Catholic Epistles on first-peter 4:15
entries.excerpt: But let none of you suffer as a murderer, etc. For he suffers as a blasphemer who, at the time of his suffering, is carried away into the injury of his persecutor.

entries.title: Commentary on first-peter 4:15
entries.excerpt: Another returns to that country whence he was banished, to perish when arrested, not now as being a Christian, but as being a criminal!

entries.title: INTRODUCTORY COMMENTARY ON 1 PETER on first-peter 4:15
entries.excerpt: Our suffering must not be like that of the thief on the cross, who suffered because he was a murderer, even though he himself confessed that Christ had done no wrong. Nor should we be like Ananias and Sapphira, who tried to steal what belonged to God. Even less should we imitate Simon Magus, who denounced the apostles to Nero and who tried to buy their gifts with gold and silver.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Peter on first-peter 4:15
entries.excerpt: "even as a bigmouth," who curiously scrutinizes what belongs to others, in order to have an occasion for slander. This, however, is the disposition of a wicked and deceitful mind, and one that is quick and ready for every evil.

entries.title: Commentary on 1 Peter on first-peter 4:15
entries.excerpt: "One who meddles in others' affairs" is he who is excessively curious about the business of others in order to have occasion for slander. But such an occupation is characteristic of an impure soul, one that is villainous and ready for every evil.
